outcome_contract.why: Staff using the rooms grid have no at-a-glance view of how many rooms are occupied tonight. They must count occupied cells across all 10 rooms manually — a slow and error-prone task when deciding whether to push discounts, adjust minimum stays, or block availability.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: A compact occupancy count strip appears on the rooms-grid page showing the number of occupied rooms vs total rooms for the current date, derived from existing booking data without additional network requests.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: `RoomsGrid.tsx` uses DS primitives (`Cluster`, `Inline`, `Stack`). `StatusLegend` pattern gives a reference for a compact strip above the grid.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Strip must use DS primitives only; no raw flex. Color choice for the strip label/number needs token-based colors only.
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Confirm strip placement (below StatusLegend, above DndProvider); define visual treatment in plan task.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Grid shows loading/error states at `RoomsGrid` level. Existing pattern: strip only renders when `!loading && error == null`.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Need empty-state (0 occupied), full-house state (all occupied), and today-outside-window state.
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Address all states in OccupancyStrip component and plan task.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Internal staff tool, no auth boundary change, no new data exposed beyond what the grid already displays.
No new data mutation or external calls. Purely derived UI.
Existing RTL suite for RoomsGrid mocks useGridData. OccupancyStrip will need its own RTL test.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: New test file required for OccupancyStrip; RoomsGrid.test may need update to assert strip renders.
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Write OccupancyStrip.test.tsx covering count display, zero, and full-house cases.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: `allRoomData: Record<string, GridReservationRow[]>` already available inside `useGridData`. The hook does not expose `allRoomData` directly — `getReservationDataForRoom` exposes per-room data. To aggregate, we call `getReservationDataForRoom` per room in `RoomsGrid`.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Count logic needs to iterate `knownRooms`, call `getReservationDataForRoom(room)` for each, inspect `periods` for today overlap and occupied status. No schema change required.
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Plan must specify the occupancy computation algorithm precisely.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: `useGridData` already memoises `allRoomData` with `useMemo`. Occupancy count is O(rooms × bookings_per_room) — bounded and cheap.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: None material; memoisation of derived count via `useMemo` is recommended.
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Add `useMemo` to count derivation in plan task.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Reception is deployed as a Cloudflare Worker. No feature flag system exists. Strip is additive; rollback = revert commit.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No feature flag needed; change is additive and visual-only.
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Document rollback = git revert in plan.

stage_payload.scope_summary: Add a compact occupancy count strip to the `/rooms-grid` page in the reception app showing how many rooms are occupied tonight (e.g. "Occupied tonight: 12 / 15 rooms"). Staff currently have no at-a-glance summary and must count occupied cells in the grid manually. The feature is a new read-only UI component inserted into `RoomsGrid.tsx`. It derives the count entirely from data already loaded by `useGridData` — no new Firebase queries or API calls are needed.
goals: Display occupied room count vs total rooms for the current date (today relative to startDate/endDate window)
Use existing hook data (`allRoomData` / `getReservationDataForRoom`) for zero additional fetches
Insert the strip below `<StatusLegend />` and above `<DndProvider>` in `RoomsGrid.tsx`
Follow DS layout primitives (`Cluster`, `Inline`, `Stack`) — no raw flex divs
non_goals: Per-day occupancy column (the dispatch mentions a "row of daily totals" but P3 scoping keeps this to tonight-only count strip)
Colour-coded daily grid (deferred to a future iteration)
New Firebase reads, API routes, or server-side data fetching
Unallocated occupants counted as occupying rooms (they have no `allocated` room)
constraints: No raw flex/grid CSS — DS primitives only (ds/enforce-layout-primitives lint rule)
Tests written for CI; no local jest execution
Writer lock required for all commits
assumptions: "Tonight" = today's date falls within `[startDate, endDate]` window; if today is outside the window, no occupancy indicator is shown (or a neutral "–" count is shown)
"Occupied" means any booking period where the period's date range overlaps today AND status is NOT `free`, `disabled`, or `16` (bags-picked-up / effectively departed)
`14` (checkout complete) is treated as occupied for tonight if the guest has not yet departed (checkout day itself is ambiguous — we count it as occupied to avoid under-counting)
key_entry_points: `apps/reception/src/components/roomgrid/RoomsGrid.tsx` — the layout host; this is where the strip will be inserted

analysis_readiness.Recommended next step: `/lp-do-analysis` — compare approach options (derive from existing hook vs new Firebase query) and select the no-new-query approach.

rehearsal_issues.Scope Area: Entry point: `RoomsGrid.tsx` layout and hook wiring

rehearsal_issues.Issues Found: None — allRoomData is internal; per-room access via getReservationDataForRoom is the correct access pattern
`"free"`, `"disabled"`, `"16"` are non-occupied. All others are occupied-state. `"14"` (checkout complete) counts as occupied for the night (conservative).
`Cluster`, `Inline`, `Stack` already imported in RoomsGrid. New component must import from same package.
OccupancyStrip should accept computed props; computation belongs in RoomsGrid or a utility to allow clean testing.

`startDate` defaults to yesterday; "today" within the window is `formatDateForInput(new Date())`. Minor: need to confirm how "today" is compared to period start/end (period end = checkout date; a guest checking out today is still counted as occupied).

process_topology.note: None: local code path only. No multi-step workflow, CI lane, approval path, or operator runbook is affected.
